pages.name: Home
pages.description: Public landing page introducing the IPTV service with quick entry points into plans, live TV, and on-demand content. Includes a global search bar and an inline sign-in form/modal for the single user.
primary_functions: Introduce the IPTV service value proposition, key features, and top plans under $20/month
Provide clear calls-to-action to view plans, go to TV Guide, browse channels, and continue to Dashboard for existing users
Expose a persistent header search bar to search for content (e.g., nature documentaries)
Highlight featured series, including nature/documentary content, linking to search.html or series.html

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click primary CTA button such as "View Plans" in the hero section
Click "Go to Dashboard" after successful sign-in or when already authenticated
Click "Watch Live TV" or "Open TV Guide" promo link
Click "Browse Channels" teaser link
Submit the global search form in the header with a text query like "nature"
Click on a featured series card in the homepage carousel/grid

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Home" item in the main header navigation
Default entry URL users land on when visiting the root of the site

pages.name: Dashboard
pages.description: Signed-in home page summarizing the user’s current plan, upcoming billing, recordings, and quick links to account management.
primary_functions: Show current base plan, add-ons, number of simultaneous streams, and key usage indicators
Provide direct links to manage plan (my_plan.html), change plan (plans.html), billing (billing.html), and settings (settings.html)
Surface upcoming recordings and quick access to TV Guide (tv_guide.html)
Act as the return destination after plan upgrades, billing changes, and other confirmations

incoming_params.description: Optional context of the last completed action (e.g., 'plan_upgrade', 'billing_change', 'parental_controls_updated') to show a status message

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "View Plan Details" or similar link in the current plan summary card
Click the "Change Plan" or "Manage Plan" link near current plan information
Click the "Billing" or "Manage Billing" shortcut card
Click profile icon > "Settings" when intending to configure parental controls
Click "TV Guide" or a quick link within the upcoming recordings list
Click a shortcut tile for "My Channels" or "Favorites" if shown on the dashboard

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Dashboard" item in the main header navigation when signed in
Linked from the Home page after successful sign-in

pages.name: Plans
pages.description: Catalog of all base IPTV subscription plans with filters and sorting for discovery and upgrades.
primary_functions: List all available base plans with core details like price, channel count, and simultaneous streams
Provide filters for monthly price (e.g., max $20), channel count (e.g., 100+ channels), and billing cycle (monthly vs yearly)
Allow sorting options such as price low-to-high to choose the most affordable plan meeting filters
Support both new plan selection and change-plan flow by letting users click 'Select Plan' to open the plan summary in my_plan.html
Enable comparing multiple plans using checkboxes and an in-page comparison view/modal showing differences (e.g., simultaneous streams)

incoming_params.description: Indicates how the page is being used, e.g., 'new_subscription' vs 'change_plan' vs 'billing_flow'
Optional default billing cycle filter ('monthly' or 'annual') when entering from the Billing page

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Select Plan" button on a plan card after filters/sort are applied and startPlanChange has created a draft change
Adjust filters or sorting; page reloads or updates URL query to reflect the current filter state

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Plans" item in the main header navigation
Context and billingCycle can be passed as query parameters when navigating from Dashboard or Billing

pages.name: My Plan
pages.description: Central page showing the user’s current plan, add-ons, and any pending changes with options to review and proceed to checkout.
primary_functions: Display current base plan details, included channels, and number of simultaneous streams
Show active add-ons (e.g., sports packages) and any pending add-on additions/removals
Serve as the 'Plan Summary' page after choosing a plan from plans.html or an add-on from addon_detail.html
Provide actions to proceed to checkout (checkout.html) to confirm new plan selections or changes
Offer quick navigation to change plan (plans.html) and manage add-ons (addons.html)

incoming_params.description: Indicates what triggered viewing the plan summary (e.g., 'plans', 'addon_detail', 'dashboard')
If 'true', highlights pending subscription changes in the summary

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Proceed to Checkout" or "Save Changes" button to confirm plan/add-on changes
Click "Change Base Plan" link from the plan summary section
Click "Manage Add-ons" link from the add-ons section

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "My Plan" item in the main header navigation
Receives context parameters when redirected from Plan selection or Add-on flows

pages.name: Add-ons
pages.description: Browse and filter subscription add-ons such as sports, movies, and international channel packages.
primary_functions: List available add-ons organized by category tabs or tiles (e.g., Sports, Movies, Kids)
Provide filters for monthly price (e.g., max $10) and quality features (e.g., HD channels checkbox)
Allow users to click into a specific sports add-on that offers 5+ HD sports channels via addon_detail.html
Indicate which add-ons are already included in the user’s current plan vs available to add

incoming_params.description: Selected add-on category tab to open by default (e.g., 'sports', 'movies')
Optional origin such as 'my_plan' to tailor breadcrumbs or back navigation

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on an add-on card or its details link
Click a quick "Add to My Plan" button on an add-on card after using addAddonToDraftSubscription

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Add-ons" item in the main header navigation
Category and origin context may be provided as query parameters from other pages

pages.name: Add-on Detail
pages.description: Detail page for a single add-on package with description, channel list, and pricing.
primary_functions: Show full description, included channels, HD/4K availability, and monthly price for a selected add-on
Highlight key copy (e.g., 'Includes 5+ HD sports channels') to match user filter expectations
Let the user add the add-on to their plan via an 'Add to My Plan' or 'Include in Subscription' button
Route to my_plan.html with the add-on included in the updated plan summary for review

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the add-on whose details are being displayed

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Add to My Plan" or "Include in Subscription" button after reviewing add-on details
Click a breadcrumb or "Back to Add-ons" link to return to the previous category listing

access_methods.description: Accessed when an add-on is selected from the Add-ons page using the addonId query parameter

pages.name: Checkout
pages.description: Unified confirmation page for purchasing a new plan, upgrading/downgrading, or confirming add-on changes.
primary_functions: Summarize the selected plan and add-ons, including pricing by billing cycle (monthly or annual)
Allow the user to choose when changes take effect (e.g., start immediately) for upgrades
Collect or confirm billing details and show any applied discounts or promo codes (when reached from billing.html or plans.html)
Provide a 'Confirm', 'Confirm Upgrade', or 'Update Plan' button to finalize changes
Direct the user back to dashboard.html or my_plan.html after successful confirmation

incoming_params.description: Origin of the checkout flow, e.g., 'my_plan', 'billing', or 'plans'
Type of subscription change being confirmed, e.g., 'plan_or_addon_change', 'billing_cycle_change'

outgoing_connections.trigger: After a successful confirmation of a standard plan upgrade/downgrade, click "Go to My Account" or "Return to Dashboard"
Click a link such as "View Updated Plan" on the confirmation screen
For billing-cycle-change flows, click "Return to Billing Overview" on the confirmation screen

access_methods.description: Reached after submitting plan/add-on/billing changes from My Plan or Billing pages
Uses 'from' and 'context' query parameters to render the correct confirmation copy

pages.name: Channels
pages.description: Channel directory for browsing all available channels with filters by genre, language, and rating.
primary_functions: List channels with key metadata (name, logo, genre, language, rating, HD/4K badges)
Support filters such as genre (e.g., Movies, News, Kids), language (e.g., English, Spanish), and rating (e.g., All Ages/TV-G)
Allow users to click on a channel to view its dedicated page in channel_detail.html
Provide entry points to add channels to favorites via quick actions or from channel_detail.html

incoming_params.description: Optional genre filter applied on load, e.g., 'movies', 'news', 'kids'
Optional language filter code (e.g., 'en', 'es') applied on load
Optional maximum rating filter such as 'all_ages' or 'tv_g'
Optional sort option value such as 'name_az', 'number_ascending', or 'popular'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a channel row or its logo/name in the channels list
Click a contextual "View Schedule" or "Open in Guide" link for a channel

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Channels" item in the main header navigation
Supports deep-linking with pre-applied filters for genre, language, rating, and sort

pages.name: Channel Detail
pages.description: Individual channel page with description, schedule preview, and favorite action.
primary_functions: Show a channel’s description, primary language, genre, and typical content (e.g., movies, news, kids)
Display a mini schedule or link to the full schedule in tv_guide.html for that channel
Enable users to add or remove the channel from favorites via an 'Add to Favorites' button or heart icon
Provide navigation back to the filtered channels list (channels.html) so users can continue selecting required favorites

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the channel whose details and mini schedule are being shown

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Back to Channels" link to return to the main channels list
Click "View Full Schedule" to open the TV Guide focused on this channel
After marking several channels as favorites, click header "Favorites" / "My Channels" link

access_methods.description: Accessed from channel listings, favorites, or TV guide using the channelId query parameter

pages.name: Favorites
pages.description: List of all channels and content the user has favorited for quick access.
primary_functions: Display all favorited channels (including the English movie, Spanish news, and all-ages kids channels the user selects)
Allow users to manage favorites (reorder or remove channels from favorites)
Provide direct links to open each channel in channel_detail.html or jump to its row in tv_guide.html
Act as the destination when the user clicks 'Favorites' or 'My Channels' after favoriting channels

incoming_params.description: Optional origin (e.g., 'channels', 'dashboard') used only for UI breadcrumbs

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a favorited channel’s name or logo in the favorites list
Click a "View in Guide" or EPG icon next to a favorite channel
Click a "Find more channels" link at the bottom of the favorites page

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Favorites" / "My Channels" item in the main header navigation

pages.name: TV Guide
pages.description: Interactive electronic program guide (EPG) showing the live schedule across channels and dates.
primary_functions: Display a time/channel grid of live programming with navigation by date (e.g., next Friday) and time slots
Allow filtering by genre such as Sports to quickly locate relevant programs
Enable users to select a program block to view program details in a popup or side panel
Provide scheduling controls to record a selected program, choose 'Keep for 7 days', and save the recording

incoming_params.description: Date to display in the guide, formatted as YYYY-MM-DD; next Friday for the recording task
Optional genre filter such as 'sports', used to narrow the guide grid
Optional starting time for the visible window, e.g., '20:00'
Optional end time for the visible window in the grid
Optional channel to focus/scroll to within the grid

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a channel name or logo from the grid to view its channel details page

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "TV Guide" item in the main header navigation
Date, genre, time range, and channelId can be passed as query parameters to deep-link into the guide

pages.name: Settings
pages.description: Account settings page with sections for parental controls and viewing preferences.
primary_functions: Provide a 'Parental Controls' section where users can create and confirm a PIN (e.g., 1234)
Allow users to set a maximum allowed content rating (e.g., TV-14) via dropdown or slider
Offer a toggle to block purchases without entering the parental control PIN
Include links back to dashboard.html and other sections like streaming quality or profile preferences (if present)

incoming_params.description: Selected settings section to open, e.g., 'parental_controls'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a "Back to Dashboard" or similar link after updating settings
Click a contextual "Need Help?" link within Settings

access_methods.description: Accessed via a profile/account icon drop-down selecting "Settings"
The 'section' query parameter focuses the desired settings tab upon load

pages.name: Billing
pages.description: Billing & payments hub for managing billing cycle, promo codes, and annual plan selection.
primary_functions: Show current billing overview including next charge date, current billing cycle, and amount
Provide controls to switch between monthly and annual billing cycles (e.g., select the 'Annual' option)
Allow entry and application of promo codes such as SAVE20, with feedback on discount applied
List available annual plans with their annual totals and allow sorting low-to-high
Enable selection of a new annual plan under $200/year and confirmation via 'Update Subscription' leading through checkout.html or an in-page confirmation
Offer a 'Return to Billing Overview' link or button to refresh the billing summary after updates

incoming_params.description: Origin of navigation to Billing, such as 'dashboard' or 'checkout'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the "Update Subscription" button after selecting an annual plan and applying promo code
Click "Return to Billing Overview" within an inline confirmation component to refresh overview state
Click a "Return to Dashboard" link from the Billing page

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Billing" item in the main header navigation
Optional 'from' parameter records the entry context into billing flows

pages.name: Search Results
pages.description: Search results page for content discovery across live and on-demand catalog.
primary_functions: Display results for queries entered in the global header search bar (e.g., 'nature')
Provide filters for resolution/quality (e.g., select 4K), content type (e.g., Series), and sorting (e.g., Most Popular)
Allow users to identify documentary content via genre or tags and click into a specific title in series.html
Support query parameters such as query=nature, resolution=4k, content_type=series, sort=most_popular in lowercase_snake_case

incoming_params.description: Search string entered in the global header search bar, e.g., 'nature'
Optional resolution filter such as '4k', 'hd', etc.
Optional content type filter such as 'series' or 'movie'
Optional genre filter, e.g., 'documentary'
Sorting method such as 'most_popular' or 'top_results'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a search result tile representing a series

access_methods.description: Accessed when the global search form is submitted from any page
Uses query, resolution, content_type, genre, and sort query parameters to construct the search call

pages.name: Series Detail
pages.description: Detail page for an on-demand series, including description, seasons, and episodes.
primary_functions: Show title metadata such as synopsis, genre (e.g., Documentary), resolution (e.g., 4K), and tags
List seasons and episodes in order with clear labels for Episode 1 and subsequent episodes
Provide 'Play' buttons for each episode, allowing the user to start playback of Episode 1 of a selected nature documentary
Link back to search results (search.html) or related content recommendations

incoming_params.description: Identifier of the series whose details and episodes are being viewed
Original search query string, used for a "Back to results" link

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click "Back to search results" when the page was reached from a search

access_methods.description: Accessed from search results or featured content using seriesId (and optionally fromQuery) query parameters

pages.name: Help & Support
pages.description: Support center with FAQs and contact options for customer assistance.
primary_functions: Provide FAQs for common tasks such as changing plans, managing add-ons, parental controls, and scheduling recordings
Offer contact options (e.g., support email, chat link, or phone number) within the same page
Link to key functional pages like billing.html, settings.html, and my_plan.html for guided help
Present troubleshooting steps for playback and account issues

incoming_params.description: FAQ section to focus on, such as 'billing', 'my_plan', 'settings', or 'tv_guide'

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a help link in the "Parental Controls" FAQ answer
Click a help link in a billing or promo code-related FAQ
Click a help link about viewing or changing your plan
Click a help link about scheduling or managing recordings

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Help" item in the main header navigation
Accessible via the "Help & Support" link in the footer

pages.name: About
pages.description: Informational page about the IPTV service provider, brand story, and trust information.
primary_functions: Describe the company background, mission, and key differentiators
Highlight partnerships, content providers, and supported devices
Build trust with users through short bios, timeline, or customer highlights

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "About" link in the site footer
Can be linked from marketing campaigns or external sites for company information

pages.name: Legal
pages.description: Consolidated legal page containing terms of service, privacy policy, and other legal notices.
primary_functions: Present the service’s terms of use in a readable format
Present the privacy policy and data handling practices
Include other regulatory or legal notices as needed (e.g., copyright, acceptable use)

incoming_params.description: Optional anchor to scroll to a specific legal document section like 'terms' or 'privacy'

access_methods.description: Accessible via the "Legal" link in the site footer

header_links.description: Public homepage for the IPTV service
Signed-in overview of current plan, recordings, and quick actions
Browse and choose base IPTV subscription plans
View and manage add-on packages such as sports and movies
Browse all available TV channels with filters
Live TV schedule grid and recording controls
View and manage your current plan and add-ons
Manage billing cycle, promo codes, and payment details
Quick access to your favorited channels

footer_links.description: Learn more about the IPTV service provider
FAQs and ways to contact support
Terms of service, privacy policy, and legal information
